heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 5.0°C) and outside temperature (down 4.5°C), more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 9%), and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 10935 lux) and cloud levels (up 781 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 15977 lux), dew point temperature (up 3.1°C), outside temperature (up 7.2°C), and cloud levels (up 1615 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 781.2m lower than yesterday, 1157.7m lower than the past week, 1614.6m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1023m minimum and 4120m maximum, with an average of 1273.2m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 2430m and 2890m.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should remain cool. Outside; prepare for cold, chilly weather ahead. Also, low humidity will be noticeable and calm and still conditions are expected. A few light raindrops may fall, the sky may alternate between sunny and overcast, and weak sunlight because of clouds. The weather may change rapidly and become worse.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (4.4°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
